title: SessionStart of either host clears the shared dev-active marker and logs a synthetic 'DEV: exit' while the other host's session may still be live in /dev
evidence: clear_dev_marker(root) runs unconditionally on every SessionStart, before the arbiter gate: it appends '[ts] | BY: session-cleanup | DEV: exit | NOTE: cleared by SessionStart (prior session ended mid-dev…)' to overrides.log and removes .codearbiter/.markers/dev-active. The marker is repo-global, but /dev is 'per-session'. With this branch's dual-host configuration (Claude and Codex sessions concurrently in one repo — the campaign's stated scenario), starting host B while host A is mid-/dev deletes A's live marker: A's statusline dev segment and the CONFIRM-09 dev staleness flow silently flip off, and the audit trail records a false DEV: exit for a session that never exited.
impact: Orphan/clobbered cross-session state: mode indication and staleness tracking for the still-running session are wrong, and the append-only audit trail contains an incorrect close event. Pre-existing for two Claude sessions, but the dual-host branch makes concurrent sessions a supported, likelier configuration.
recommendation: Make the marker session-scoped (embed the session id in the marker name or content and only clear markers owned by dead sessions), or gate the startup clear on marker age (only clear when stale past the CONFIRM-09 window) so a freshly-active /dev in another session survives.
acceptance_criteria: Starting a second session while another session is <30min into /dev does not remove the marker or append DEV: exit
An orphaned marker from a genuinely ended session is still cleaned up
